records.ety: [AS. cluster, clyster; cf. LG. kluster (also Sw. & Dan. klase a cluster of grapes, D. klissen to be entangled?.)]

defs: A number of things of the same kind growing together; a bunch.
quotes: Her deeds were like great clusters of ripe grapes, Which load the bunches of the fruitful vine.

defs: A number of similar things collected together or lying contiguous; a group; as, a cluster of islands.
A number of individuals grouped together or collected in one place; a crowd; a mob.
quotes: As bees . . . Pour forth their populous youth about the hive In clusters.
We loved him; but, like beasts And cowardly nobles, gave way unto your clusters, Who did hoot him out o' the city.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Clustered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Clustering.]

defs: To grow in clusters or assemble in groups; to gather or unite in a cluster or clusters.
quotes: His sunny hair Cluster'd about his temples, like a god's.
The princes of the country clustering together.

defs: To collect into a cluster or clusters; to gather into a bunch or close body.
quotes: Not less the bee would range her cells, . . . The foxglove cluster dappled bells.
Or from the forest falls the clustered snow.
